steps.title: Identify which works the notes call most famous
steps.content: The notes explicitly identify E.M. Forster's most famous novels as A Room with a View (1908) and Howards End (1910). To provide a specific example of one of his most famous works, the answer must reference one of these two novels.

steps.title: Find the choice that names one of these famous works
steps.content:
Choice C identifies A Room with a View and calls it “one of E.M.

Forster's most famous works.” The other choices either provide only general information about Forster without naming a specific famous work (A), or name “Albergo Empedocle,” which the notes describe as his first published work, not one of his most famous (B and D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
